scope: MiMo 2.5-pro highout32k full236 EVAS/Spectre conformance refresh

resolved_l0_findings.finding: waveform parity gate on resettable counter divider
resolved_l0_findings.previous_evidence: pre-v4 full236 triage showed one waveform parity gate on vbm1_resettable_counter_divider_tb
resolved_l0_findings.root_cause: EVAS treated a Spectre pulse source with no period as a DC-high source. Spectre applies a nonperiodic one-shot pulse: the source stays at val0 until delay, rises once to val1, and stays there unless width is specified.
resolved_l0_findings.fix: Implement nonperiodic pulse semantics in EVAS pulse() and _add_spectre_source(), including one-shot width fallback.
resolved_l0_findings.current_status: resolved; current full236 v4 has zero waveform parity gate rows

resolved_l0_findings.finding: Spectre PASS / EVAS FAIL on calibration deadband boundary
resolved_l0_findings.previous_evidence: pre-v4 mismatch rerun showed a calibration-deadband boundary false negative
resolved_l0_findings.root_cause: Both simulators followed the same trim sequence, but an exact 0.120 V span boundary could land on opposite sides of the threshold due to transition sampling granularity.
resolved_l0_findings.fix: Relax the boundary by 1e-6 V in the release calibration-loop checker.
resolved_l0_findings.current_status: resolved; current full236 v4 has zero Spectre PASS / EVAS FAIL rows

resolved_l0_findings.finding: Spectre PASS / EVAS FAIL on pipeline ADC stage bugfix
resolved_l0_findings.previous_evidence: pre-v4 full236 triage showed Spectre PASS / EVAS FAIL on vbr1_l1_pipeline_adc_stage:bugfix
resolved_l0_findings.root_cause: EVAS used a case-insensitive preflight check for parameter/port collisions. Verilog-A identifiers are case-sensitive, so port VDD and parameter vdd are distinct and Spectre accepts the candidate.
resolved_l0_findings.fix: Make the parameter/port collision preflight case-sensitive and add a regression that permits case-distinct supply identifiers.
resolved_l0_findings.current_status: resolved; current full236 v4 has zero Spectre PASS / EVAS FAIL rows

resolved_l0_findings.finding: EVAS accepted Spectre-rejected PWL inline arithmetic and too-few instance terminals
resolved_l0_findings.previous_evidence: pre-v4 full236 triage exposed PWL inline arithmetic and positional arity false positives
resolved_l0_findings.root_cause: EVAS accepted PWL wave tokens such as 30n+10p and positional instances with fewer terminals than the target Verilog-A module. Spectre rejects both.
resolved_l0_findings.fix: Reject inline arithmetic inside PWL wave=[...] and validate expanded positional instance arity before simulation.
resolved_l0_findings.current_status: resolved; current full236 v4 has zero EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL rows

conclusion: The remaining parity-gate investigation resolved as EVAS source semantics plus one checker-window calibration case; after current EVAS v4, the full236 MiMo 2.5-pro highout32k run has no EVAS/Spectre binary mismatches and no waveform parity gate rows.
